item.title: Ryanair passenger says he was nearly sucked out of broken window during flight
item.description: A 61-year-old Serbian businessman who was traveling on a Ryanair flight says he was almost sucked out of the plane when a window broke. Ryanair contests parts of the man's account. CBS News' Tom Hanson has more.

item.title: Sneak peek: Justine's Voice
item.description: ENCORE: After a teenager is strangled and buried alive, her family takes on the fight to keep her convicted killers behind bars. "We're her voice" says Justine's sister. "48 Hours" correspondent Natalie Morales reports Saturday, July 25 at 9/8c on CBS and streaming on Paramount+.

item.title: Trump threatens to attack Iran's infrastructure over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump in a social media post threatened to attack Iran's infrastructure if the Iranian military shoots at ships in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Charlie D'Agata have more.

item.title: H.R. McMaster on Hegseth's Iran war funding request, why Pickaxe Mountain is a potential target
item.description: Retired Lt. Gen. H.R. McMaster weighs in on the dignified transfer of U.S. troops killed in the Iranian strikes, Defense Secretary Hegseth's military strategy and national security preparedness.

item.title: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine's top general after days of protests
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelenskyy announced that he will replace​ the commander in chief of the armed forces as a government shake-up continues.

item.title: Trump threatens to bomb a bridge or power plant for every ship Iran attacks
item.description: The Pentagon keeps insisting the Strait of Hormuz is open, but with Iranian attacks continuing, one shipping insider says "nothing is going through."

item.title: Trump attends dignified transfer of soldiers killed in Iranian attacks
item.description: President Trump attended a dignified transfer honoring the four U.S. Army soldiers killed in the Middle East in recent days during the ongoing war with Iran.

item.title: U.S. reaches deal with Saudis for nuclear energy commercial agreements
item.description: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia are expected to sign a deal for U.S. companies to run nuclear power for the Saudis, potentially allowing the Saudis to enrich their own fuel for nuclear reactors.

item.title: Lung cancer in nonsmokers: "A women's health crisis"
item.description: Lung cancer is the leading cause of cancer death in American women — and a growing share of new cases are in women who've never smoked.

item.title: Why Zelenskyy fired Ukraine's top general
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy fired Gen. Oleksandr Syrskyi on Tuesday amid mass protests and cabinet turmoil. CBS News' Aidan Stretch has more.

item.title: What to expect from the next Fed meeting amid economic strains of Iran war
item.description: The Federal Reserve is set to meet next week amid economic strains from the renewed and ongoing conflict with Iran, including higher oil prices. Ann Berry, the CEO of Threadneedle, joins with more on what to expect.

item.title: What Nolan Wells' preliminary independent autopsy findings revealed about his death
item.description: Mississippi teen Nolan Wells vanished while on a Fourth of July boat trip with a group of friends. His body was found days later. Attorney Ben Crump shared the results of the teen's preliminary independent autopsy commissioned by Wells' family. CBS News' Skyler Henry has the latest, and legal contributor Jessica Levision joins to discuss.

item.title: Nolan Wells' cause of death undetermined, family's autopsy shows
item.description: Nolan Wells, 18, was found dead just off Horn Island in Mississippi after enjoying a day out with friends on July 4.

item.title: Just bought a house? Here's when to expect the big HVAC bill.
item.description: Your home's biggest repair bill may arrive sooner than expected. Here's how to prepare for HVAC costs.

item.title: More than 9 million student loan borrowers now in default
item.description: More than 9 million student loan borrowers are now in default after the Trump administration wiped out Biden-era repayment plans. Danielle Douglas-Gabriel, a national higher education reporter for The Washington Post, joins with more.

item.title: Kalshi debuts a midterm election hub to predict race outcomes
item.description: The site claims its hub will provide accurate insights into election outcomes based on traders' bets.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha closes in on Gulf Coast, expected to bring major flooding
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is expected to hit Louisiana and other Gulf states with major flooding as it continues to travel toward land. CBS News' Lana Zak has more.

item.title: Nolan Wells' preliminary independent autopsy results read by attorney Ben Crump
item.description: The body of Mississippi teen Nolan Wells was discovered days after he went missing during a Fourth of July boat trip. Attorney Ben Crump read the results of a preliminary independent autopsy commissioned by Wells' family to the public.

item.title: Wreckage of Pan Am plane found in ocean 74 years after deadly crash
item.description: The crash in 1952 killed 52 people and led to requirements for the passenger safety briefings given on every commercial flight.

item.title: Rubio says Iran isn't serious about negotiations; Hegseth says war has cost $37.5 billion
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio told reporters that while the U.S. is open to negotiations with Iran, Tehran doesn't seem serious about a solution. Meanwhile, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified Tuesday that the war has cost $37.5 billion so far. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Aaron Navarro have the latest.

item.title: FDA recalls antihistamine tablets. Here are the affected products.
item.description: A generic version of the allergy medication Zyrtec is under recall across the U.S. over cross-contamination concerns.

item.title: Democrats probe DOJ opinion that eases institutionalization of mentally ill
item.description: Democrats in the Senate and House are investigating the origins of a DOJ legal opinion that would make it easier for states to keep people with mental illness in institutions, rather than helping them to live at home.

item.title: Behind Norah O'Donnell's new podcast "Healthful"
item.description: Norah O'Donnell talks about her new podcast, "Healthful with Norah O'Donnell," which is about providing helpful and trusted information on women's health. Each week, she speaks with leading experts on everything from mental health to fitness.

item.title: Woman recovering from cyclosporiasis says she's still "really weak" as CDC marks over 4,000 cases
item.description: A woman who had cyclosporiasis said the parasite made her "really weak." There are more than 4,000 confirmed cases, according to the Centers for Disease Control. CBS News' Carter Evans has more.

item.title: Melinda French Gates: It Took Three Doctors to Diagnose My Perimenopause
item.description:
Melinda French Gates is focusing her money and attention on improving women's healthcare in America. In Healthful's first episode, French Gates tells Norah O'Donnell why it took three doctors to catch her perimenopause and shares the three simple things that keep her healthier than ever.
We'd love to hear what topics you want covered on Healthful. Text Norah at 202-217-1107

item.title: The Rise of Lung Cancer in Women and Never-Smokers
item.description:
Lung cancer has surpassed breast cancer as the leading cause of cancer deaths in American women—and a growing share of new cases are in women who have never touched a cigarette. Anne Wojcicki's sister, former YouTube CEO Susan Wojcicki, was one of them. In this episode of HEALTHFUL, Norah O'Donnell sits down with Anne, Dr. Kim Sandler, Director of the Vanderbilt Lung Screening Program, and Shira Kupperman Boehler—a lifelong nonsmoker and daily runner diagnosed with lung cancer at 44—to discuss why lung cancer is becoming one of the most urgent, and least understood, threats to women's health.
We'd love to hear what topics you want covered on Healthful. Text Norah at 202-217-1107

item.title: OpenAI says its technology, on its own, hacked another AI company
item.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI says its artificial intelligence system hacked into another AI company on its own in what the company called an "unprecedented cyber incident."

item.title: Andy Burnham takes the reins as U.K.'s 7th prime minister in a decade
item.description: Having been asked by King Charles III to form a new government, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham has become Britain's 7th prime minister in a decade.

item.title: "48 Hours" show schedule
item.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

item.title: Introducing Healthful with Norah O'Donnell
item.description:
Award-winning journalist Norah O'Donnell has spent decades asking questions in pursuit of the truth. Now she's using that passion to bring women information on topics that matter most to them - and that have long been ignored by the medical world.
Healthful is the podcast that puts women's health first. Norah brings you the doctors, the scientists, and the innovators who are at the forefront on reproductive health, menopause, cancer and more. Forget miracle cures, vague advice and fear-mongering and get real information you can actually use. And yes, Norah will divulge her own wellness secrets (blueberries and Botox among them). New episodes available every Wednesday.
